description: Security scan baseline — accepted risks, confirmed false positives, and deferred findings. Managed by the operator after each security-scan triage run. The commission step produces baseline-updates.md with recommended changes.

entries.reason: Parameterized ? placeholders only — placeholders array contains literal '?' strings, real values passed via variadic args
Hardcoded SQL fragments with ? placeholders — conditions slice contains static strings, user values in parameterized args
Explicit filepath.Clean + HasPrefix path traversal guard in code
Local CLI tool — all 88 exec.Command calls use variadic args (not sh -c), inputs from internal state (SOL_HOME, SQLite, TOML). No untrusted input path.
All 75 file-path-via-variable instances use paths rooted under SOL_HOME, built from validated identifiers. No network input.
72 MkdirAll(0755) calls — standard Go convention for directories in single-user CLI tool. Sensitive files within use 0600.
Cast() TOCTOU explicitly mitigated with double-check-after-lock pattern at dispatch.go:200-207
Deliberate //nolint:errcheck — ROLLBACK on broken connection, GC handles cleanup
Same safe parameterized-placeholder pattern as FP-1 — placeholders[i] = "?" with literal strings only, real values passed via variadic args
PID file opened with 0644 — appropriate for single-user CLI tool, no sensitive data. (Marked stale_keep_for_now 2026-04-28: no current matching call site, retained in case scanner re-flags.)

Test-helper SetupEnv writes JSON contracts under t.TempDir() — not reachable in production.
Build-time JSON schema generator output — developer-only codegen tool, not linked into bin/sol.
Build-time API doc generator output — developer-only tool, not linked into bin/sol.

Credential visibility in ps is inherent to shell-based env injection. Standard tradeoff for tmux-based session management. Alternative approaches (tmux set-environment, temp file) introduce different risks. Single-user deployment model.
Symlink following with handle validation. Requires SOL_HOME write access to exploit. filepath.Rel + SplitN provides defense-in-depth.
TOCTOU in CLI-driven world import. Narrow window, non-security consequence (overwrite, not escalation).

Caller-enforced locking contract documented in package header. All current callers comply. Internal package with small, well-defined call surface. Adding internal locking would change concurrency model.
